claim: Specialized parallel agents solve the hallucination problem that occurs when a single agent gets overwhelmed with instructions and tools
actionable: Split monolithic AI agents into focused sub-agents with narrow roles to prevent degradation in performance as task complexity grows
source_title: 10x Your AI Agents with this ONE Agent Architecture

claim: LLMs perform better with narrow, focused goals than broad multi-purpose objectives; specialization improves individual agent quality
actionable: Design each agent with a single, clearly-defined responsibility and minimal instruction set rather than trying to handle multiple domains in one agent
source_title: 10x Your AI Agents with this ONE Agent Architecture

claim: Pydantic AI structured outputs guarantee response format, enabling reliable downstream processing in multi-agent systems
actionable: Use Pydantic AI's result_type parameter to enforce JSON schema validation in agents that feed into pipelines or other agents
source_title: 10x Your AI Agents with this ONE Agent Architecture

claim: LangGraph allows true parallel execution of agents via conditional edges that return multiple nodes simultaneously
actionable: In LangGraph, return a list of node names from a routing function to execute them in true parallel, not sequentially
source_title: 10x Your AI Agents with this ONE Agent Architecture

claim: A synthesizer/aggregator agent that combines outputs from parallel agents creates superior final results vs. simple concatenation
actionable: After parallel agents complete work, route outputs through a final synthesis agent that reason over and combines recommendations rather than raw output
source_title: 10x Your AI Agents with this ONE Agent Architecture

claim: Gate-keeping agent that validates input completeness before invoking downstream agents prevents hallucination from missing context
actionable: Use a dedicated information-gathering agent with structured output to validate all required parameters before triggering specialized agents
source_title: 10x Your AI Agents with this ONE Agent Architecture

claim: Pydantic AI tools are just Python functions wrapped with decorators; docstrings serve as the LLM's instruction for when to invoke them
actionable: Write comprehensive docstrings for tool functions that explain purpose and parameters; this is your LLM's decision logic for tool invocation
source_title: 10x Your AI Agents with this ONE Agent Architecture

claim: Run agents synchronously for non-streamed outputs and use RunStream only for streamed/interactive output to avoid over-complicating code
actionable: Use synchronous agent invocation by default; only switch to RunStream when you need real-time token streaming to the user interface
source_title: 10x Your AI Agents with this ONE Agent Architecture

claim: LangGraph human-in-the-loop interrupts allow pausing execution at any point to wait for user input without losing context
actionable: Use LangGraph's interrupt feature to pause agent execution and collect user confirmation/input before proceeding to next steps
source_title: 10x Your AI Agents with this ONE Agent Architecture
